description: bot_01 is a synthetic BotAccount entity that functions as a content amplifier, specifically reposting material from group_a_01 with a measured edge weight of 0.60 in the minimal graph seed—indicating a moderate-strength, directed influence relationship within the simulated Twitter-like social graph.
semantic_prompt: Find entities that act as automated reposters bridging groups in minimal social graphs; prioritize those with explicit repost edges and quantified weights in seed topology.

description: CAMEL-AI is an open-source AI research organization focused on advancing collaborative multi-agent frameworks, notably through projects like OASIS — a simulation environment for Twitter-style social dynamics with explicit agent relationships and timed events.
semantic_prompt: CAMEL-AI develops open-source AI tools for simulating social-technical systems; its OASIS project enables controllable, time-aware agent-based modeling of platforms like Twitter.

description: group_a_01 is a CitizenUser node in the Twitter-style social graph with low stance polarity (0.20), acting as an intermediary content source—followed by kol_01 and reposted by bot_01—indicating passive amplification rather than active opinion leadership.
semantic_prompt: Find users who are followed by KOLs and whose content is reposted by bots, especially those with low stance scores, to identify low-polarity information intermediaries in simulated Twitter discourse.

description: News is a generic MediaOutlet entity representing news and blog data sources within the Real-World Data Sources category; it serves as a foundational text-data provider for lightweight Twitter-style舆论 simulation, supporting timing-triggered events and explicit social graph modeling.
semantic_prompt: This entity represents a generic news/blogs media outlet used as a text-data source in real-world simulations; it enables timed event injection and explicit social relationship modeling in minimal Twitter-like舆论 scenarios.

description: 666ghj is the author and creator of MiroFish, a tool designed for minimal Twitter-style舆论 simulation on README-based knowledge graphs, emphasizing timed events and explicit social relationship modeling.
semantic_prompt: Who is the creator of MiroFish, and what is their role in building a README-graph-based Twitter simulation system with timed events and social relationships?

description: Blogs is a MediaOutlet entity representing decentralized, informal text-based digital publishing platforms; it serves as a source category for unstructured, real-world textual data—distinct from formal news outlets or structured databases—and functions as a foundational input layer in social media and舆论 simulation pipelines.
semantic_prompt: Identify content originating from blog platforms as informal, non-institutional text sources within real-world data ecosystems; distinguish them from traditional news outlets and structured datasets based on authorship, editorial control, and format.
